channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Infantino's 'time is up' with Fifa - La Liga boss
item.description: La Liga president Javier Tebas calls for Fifa president Gianni Infantino to quit, and claims the governing body is "destroying the football industry".

item.title: What could Burnham as PM mean for football?
item.description: Andy Burnham is only into his second day as the UK's Prime Minister, but already people are wondering what this could mean for sport.

item.title: Bournemouth midfielder Scott rejects new contract
item.description: Bournemouth midfielder Alex Scott rejects a new contract amid growing Premier League interest in the England Under-21 international.

item.title: The country that doesn't play football ... yet
item.description: Nauru is the only nation to have never played an official international football match and hardly anyone there plays the game. Is that about to change?

item.title: Ferguson says Keegan built Newcastle's greatest team
item.description: Former Manchester United manager Sir Alex Ferguson says Kevin Keegan "carried the love of the game on his sleeve" as he paid tribute to his former rival.

item.title: Keeper Martinez may 'step aside' from Argentina
item.description: Argentina goalkeeper Emi Martinez says he is considering whether it is "time to step aside" from international football after his side's 1-0 defeat to Spain in the World Cup final.

item.title: Why Newcastle are changing club badge
item.description: Newcastle United supporters are set to vote on a new crest as the club look to change their badge for the first time since 1988.

item.title: Fernandez defends Argentina as Messi questions remain
item.description: Midfielder Enzo Fernandez claims Argentina represented their nation "in the best possible way" in the World Cup.

item.title: Why England teenager Parkinson has a mindset coach
item.description: Why England teenager Erica Meg Parkinson has a mindset coach who has worked with the All Blacks and ex-England cricketer Ashley Giles.

item.title: Fake kits worth £18m seized by police during World Cup
item.description: Counterfeit football shirts worth over £18m were seized in the UK during the course of the World Cup because of safety concerns, with the Fire Service warning the outfits can be a danger to those who wear them.

item.title: 'I'm proud of it' - Keegan looks back on career highlights and legacy
item.description: Kevin Keegan talks to Football Focus' Dan Walker in 2018 about some of the standout moments from his career - from winning World Player of the Year twice, to managing England and the iconic "I would love it" remark from the 1995–96 season.

item.title: Keegan's double inspires Liverpool to 1974 FA Cup glory
item.description: Kevin Keegan scores twice as Liverpool defeat Newcastle United 3-0 in the 1974 FA Cup final.

item.title: Watch the BBC's closing World Cup montage
item.description: Watch the closing montage of the BBC's coverage of the 2026 Fifa World Cup featuring all the highs, lows and colour from the biggest World Cup ever.

item.title: Argentina boss Scaloni leaves press conference in tears
item.description: Argentina manager Lionel Scaloni leaves his post-match news conference in tears when talking about his future after Sunday's World Cup final defeat by Spain.

item.title: 'A date with history and we got there first' - Spain react to World Cup win
item.description: Spain manager Luis de la Fuente and his players react to winning their second World Cup title after beating Argentina 1-0 after extra time in the final.

item.title: Dominant Spain beat Argentina to win World Cup after extra time
item.description: Ferran Torres scores the only goal of the game as Spain dominate Argentina 1-0, to win their second FIFA World Cup at the New York New Jersey Stadium.

item.title: Ex-Villa striker Duran joins seventh club aged 22
item.description: Former Aston Villa striker Jhon Duran joins Benfica on a season-long loan from Saudi Arabian side Al-Nassr.

item.title: Fifa opens investigation into Argentina post-final trouble - what happens next?
item.description: Argentina will face an investigation into the ugly scenes at the end of the World Cup final, Fifa has confirmed.

item.title: Man Utd and Arsenal fight over Kone - Tuesday's gossip
item.description: Manchester United and Arsenal fight over France's Manu Kone, Nico Williams could be heading to the Premier League champions, and West Ham reject latest Crysencio Summerville offer.

item.title: Gifted, charismatic and passionate amid highs and lows - Keegan obituary
item.description: The positivity and vibrancy Kevin Keegan brought made him a cherished figure within the game, writes chief football writer Phil McNulty.

item.title: Guardiola in reported talks with Italy - would England come calling?
item.description: Former Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola, a fluent Italian speaker, is a contender for the vacant Italy head coach job.

item.title: Why World Cup can be game-changer for ambitious MLS
item.description: Can the success of the 2026 World Cup help MLS become one of the world's elite leagues?

item.title: Thousands of websites taken down for illegal World Cup streams
item.description: More than 1,000 illegal streaming sites were shut down in the US during the tournament, with a similar amount blocked in Colombia.

item.title: Everton agree deal to sign Leicester’s Rantala
item.description: Everton agree a deal to sign Finland international Jutta Rantala from Leicester City.

item.title: Who am I? Guess Premier League star No 1
item.description: Work out the identity of today's player in as few attempts as possible.

item.title: Why is the Community Shield being held in Wales?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains why the 2026 Community Shield is being held in Wales.

item.title: Japan goalkeeper Okuma signs for Aston Villa
item.description: Aston Villa announce the signing of 21-year-old Japan goalkeeper Akane Okuma.

item.title: 'The reaction is terrible' - World Cup final turns ugly at full-time
item.description: The frustration of some Argentina players boils over at the end of the 2026 World Cup final, after the 2022 winners are beaten by Spain in extra time.

item.title: Mbappe wins race for the 2026 World Cup golden boot
item.description: All the goals from the race for the 2026 World Cup golden boot as Kylian Mbappe leads the way from Lionel Messi, Jude Bellingham and Erling Haaland.

item.title: Spain's Torres scores only goal of World Cup final
item.description: Ferran Torres breaks the deadlock, scoring in extra time for Spain in the World Cup final against Argentina at the New York New Jersey Stadium.

item.title: Stars shine at the World Cup final half-time show
item.description: Madonna, BTS, Justin Bieber, and Shakira entertain the crowd at half-time in the World Cup final, with a special appearance from Ronaldo and Ronaldinho at the New York New Jersey Stadium.

item.title: England beat France in 10-goal thriller to win third-place play-off
item.description: Bukayo Saka scores a hat-trick as England beat France 6-4 in a thriller to record their best men's World Cup finish since 1966.

item.title: Brilliant first half, turbulent second - Tuchel
item.description: England manager praises his players as England beat France 6-4 for third place in the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Rice fires England into early lead against France
item.description: Captain Declan Rice gives England an early lead, scoring in the third minute against France in the World Cup third-place play-off at Miami Stadium.

item.title: What will Rogers bring to Alonso's Chelsea?
item.description: Chelsea's £117m move for Aston Villa forward Morgan Rogers is a genuine transfer window shock, but how did they do it and what does it mean for the rest of the squad?

item.title: Messi says 'pain is immense' after losing World Cup final
item.description: Lionel Messi says "the pain is immense" and that it "will take time to heal" after Argentina lost 1-0 to Spain in the World Cup final.

item.title: Thank you for making this a World Cup to remember on the BBC
item.description: BBC Director of Sport Alex Kay-Jelski's message as BBC Sport delivers a record-breaking World Cup across TV, radio, online and social.

item.title: Tottenham sign France defender Sombath from Lyon
item.description: Tottenham have signed France international Alice Sombath for a club record fee from Lyon.

item.title: Why are Premier League transfer fees soaring?
item.description: With Morgan Rogers set to become the third £100m-plus Premier League transfer of the summer, BBC Sport looks at how the market keeps growing.

item.title: All you need to know about Wafcon 2026
item.description: Find out the favourites, format and kick-off times for the delayed 2026 Women's Africa Cup of Nations in Morocco, which starts on 26 July.

item.title: How to follow the new football season on BBC Sport
item.description: When the World Cup ends we'll still have loads of great content every day as we build up to the new football season.

item.title: Best goal, best player, best fans - BBC reporters' World Cup awards
item.description: BBC Sport reporters pick their best moments of the tournament - and you can vote for yours.

item.title: Lamine Yamal 'completes football' at 19 as Argentina betray Messi
item.description: Spain's Lamine Yamal takes the glory of World Cup victory as the great Lionel Messi's evening ends in tears.

item.title: 'I turned on Ceefax, saw we'd drawn Red Star Belgrade, and almost fainted'
item.description: As Irish Premiership champions Larne prepare to face Red Star Belgrade in Champions League qualifying on Tuesday, ex-Portadown defender Alfie Stewart reflects on facing the then European Cup holders 35 years ago.

item.title: How Villa's record signing Manzambi can spearhead new era
item.description: It is a new era for Aston Villa - and Johan Manzambi, who has just joined in a club record deal worth more than £50m - will be one of the poster boys.

item.title: More games, more controversy - good and bad of biggest World Cup yet
item.description: From high ticket prices to hydration breaks, new laws and political interventions, there has rarely been a dull moment at the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: All done deals in July 2026
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: What is the World Cup prize money?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team break down the prize money on offer at the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: What are the key dates for the 2026-27 football season?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything explains the full list of key dates for the 2026-27 men's football season

item.title: Should teams bring on players just for penalty shootouts?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at the numbers behind late substitutes taking penalties in shootouts.

item.title: Who has been eliminated from the World Cup?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team lists the full set of nations who have been eliminated from the 2026 World Cup so far

item.title: How are the TV picks decided for the World Cup's round of 32?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains the complexities behind television selections for the 2026 World Cup's round of 32 fixtures

item.title: Is five-match ban for Qatar's Madibo excessive?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team look at whether Madibo's punishment for his challenge on Kone was proportionate.

item.title: Who is the African World Cup goalscorer older than Ronaldo and Messi?
item.description: While Cristiano Ronaldo and Lionel Messi appear to be in a personal battle to rewrite the World Cup record books, there is one landmark that appears beyond them.

item.title: Ipswich open talks for Celtic star Maeda - gossip
item.description: Newly-promoted Premier League side keen on Celtic forward, with Scottish champions lining up Egypt winger as a possible replacement.

item.title: Hearts agree deal to sign Forest Green captain Mendy
item.description: Forest Green Rovers captain Laurent Mendy is set to become Hearts' 10th summer signing.

item.title: Scottish quartet learn possible European opponents
item.description: Hearts, Rangers, Motherwell and Hibernian discover their pathways as the third qualifying round draws are made for the Champions League, Europa League and Conference League.

item.title: Swansea close in on deal for Motherwell's Just
item.description: Swansea City hope to tie up a deal for Motherwell's Elijah Just after he impressed for New Zealand at the World Cup.

item.title: Who has your Premiership club brought in & let go this summer?
item.description: Your one-stop shop for all the ins and outs in the Scottish Premiership during the 2026 summer transfer window.

item.title: Chelsea sign Man Utd striker Malard for £850,000
item.description: France forward Melvine Malard links up again with Chelsea manager Sonia Bompastor, who gave her her debut at Lyon.

item.title: Liverpool sign midfielder Itamura from Feyenoord
item.description: Liverpool sign attacking midfielder Mao Itamura from Feyenoord.

item.title: In spotlight but shunning limelight - Olise's unique rise to top
item.description: A footballer who dislikes too much media attention, but Michael Olise might not have much choice if he carries on his World Cup heroics.

item.title: How Cornish miners brought football to Mexico
item.description: As Mexico prepares to become the first country to host or co-host the World Cup three times, BBC Sport looks at its Cornish footballing roots.

item.title: The summer a golden generation lost its shine
item.description: The summer of 2006 was meant to be England's moment. A world-class squad, celebrity partners and confident fans - the hype was huge and the fall was hard.

item.title: How Toone is navigating grief through football
item.description: England and Manchester United midfielder Ella Toone opens up about grief, football and preparing for her wedding without her dad in a new BBC documentary.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.
